content:
REG# 003
CLEARANCE
PSI
LEVEL-PSI"APEIROS"
CLASSIFICATION:
HYPATIA
DISRUPTION CLASS: EVLOGIA 3
RISK CLASS: CAUTION 3
PHYSICAL DESCRIPTION

Nex Veridis has a skull with horns for a head, is 6'0 tall (1.83m), weighs 156.6lbs (70.8kg), usually wears professional and/or rich attire. May also wear an Exosuit, because why not?

PSYCHOLOGICAL DESCRIPTION

Nex Veridis seems to be mentally stable, very clever and smart, usually speaks Old English and/or Shakespearean, at times acts odd within the HQ. If angered, it may result in some injuries.

ABILITIES

Transmutation
Pyromancy
Super Strength
Visions (Ability to see the future)
Teleportation
Possible Reality Bender
Ability to summon an omnipresent bird (specifically a raven, and isn't able to be seen.) as a memetic effect

WEAKNESSES

Cynophobia (Fear of Dogs)
Allergic to salt (Sodium base)?
Certain Special Bugs . . . (Not Mantis)
Reality Anchors (Slightly, not full weakness)

// BACKGROUND [IN SOMEONE'S THOUGHTS . . .]

Born from depths of time, Nex Veridis had come to life, although the actual birth date and place of birth remain undisclosed. Our researchers have been trying their best at finding out Nex's true origin. We know he's witnessed the birth and death of Christ, meaning he's an ancient being. He saw the Dark Plague causing chaos around Europe and survived, but might've also died? If he's dead, how come he's still alive? Can he come back to life at will and still remember all he's witnessed? He may be considered an anomaly, a humanoid with powers out of this world. He might've as well seen the big bang, but that wouldn't be possible if we know his origin came from Earth itself. His knowledge and understanding of humanity is incomparable to ours. He's had interactions with many smart people like him, for example, Pythagoras himself. He saw Rome being built and later on, collapsing on itself. But now, let me tell you his true story.

Nex Veridis did come from the depths of time, documented to be found in 259 B.C. He was originally found and discovered in Europe, most specifically in the United Kingdom, England, London, according to nowadays's map (1994), spending most of his life partaking in major historical events, as mentioned before. During his time witnessing these events, he somehow survived great pandemics that occurred in Europe during that time. His name comes from the Latin words "Death (Nex) Blooms (Veridis)". He was very smart for his time in Europe although not participating in any schools that existed before Christ. He did finish his schooling in the 1960s before re-taking it 30 years later, because otherwise he wouldn't be able to get a job to make money in the current society. Nex later decided to join Pandora's Acolytes, in which he joined as a trial low rank, later on becoming the current Director after Gamma died.

// DIRECTORSHIP AND CONDUCT

After Gamma died and Nex became the new Director, people have started noticing that he speaks and writes in Shakespearean or Old English, mostly due to his time in England, during which a lot of people spoke Old English. You may be asking yourself why Nex doesn't speak current English. It's mostly due to respect and honor, since he used to read a lot of William Shakespeare's work, and also because it is what stuck with him the most. To him, current English is like gibberish, although he understands what people are saying to him.

He does have a loved one, "Katherine Woods," a British woman he'd fall for, and she's charming. How can an anomaly fall in love? It's a question of being human. Although he is not a human, and mostly because of his time on Earth, he's seen lots and lots of couples. He understands what love is. It's not just staying close to one another or saying bitter-sweet words to each other. That's not love, it's obsession. Nex is charismatic and cares for others as he'd care for himself.

As the company's current owner, he knows what to do with the company, as well as continuously working on the Advanced Reality Anchor project. He's not a reality bender or anything. He's smart, clever, knows what he's doing. The Advanced Reality Anchor is to stop the O5 Council from ever finding the headquarters, and also, for them to be able to contain the anomalies.

The current anomalies hosted in the quarters are currently unknown, but it does entertain him watching how they're captured, also partaking himself in the capturing of anomalies. But there's one that interests him a lot, like really a lot. It's a Mantis-looking humanoid, although a bit scary to him. He's been able to befriend him. Unlike other anomalies, Mantis is the one who piqued his interest, wanting to know more about his story. Even then, it's just a matter of analyzing him.

// OBSERVATIONS FROM UNKNOWN PERSONNEL

He has a few weaknesses. He's scared of dogs, mostly because of his skull. Dogs like bones most of the time. He's also shown to have some intolerance to specifically salt, and it is mostly because of Sodium. How do we know this? It's odd, right? It's just in case something happens. Just in case.

We have vigorously looked for more information about him in his office. This isn't the end. In the most recent weaponry delivery, the guns weren't normal at all. They looked like pens. Well, I'm not sure why Nex would want to order loads of pens, but this is supposed to be weaponry, right? If it is a weaponry delivery, they should fire bullets, right? Well, quite after my supervisor left, I clicked the button of the pen. And weirdly enough, it fired a bullet. When I went to check the bullet casing on the floor, it wasn't there anymore, as if something had taken it. Because there was a small trail of leftover gunpowder that was inside the bullet, I saw a few shoe marks, business shoe footmarks. Almost nobody uses this type of shoe in the headquarters other than the Director. I followed the trail and it went through a wall. I looked and touched the walls to see if there was something hidden, but there wasn't. I remembered there was a strong smell of gunpowder coming out of the door of the Director's office. I assume he's doing something with the gunpowder, or some other vigorous thing in his office. It's really weird his love for gunpowder. A lot of people within the quarters have been weirded out by this. But despite this, Nex is a really chill guy, people like talking to him regardless of what he does.

How did he even originate in this world? Is he containing himself? Because if there were no more traces of the gunpowder other than his office, he could've very well teleported to his office. But he is still adamant about the Advanced Reality Anchor. Does he know it'll stop most of his powers if he's really a reality bender? If he's smart and clever, he probably knows a way to go around this problem, right?

Also, while scavenging around his office, I found a few videotapes, each one of them labelled as Interview with a series of numbers after the word. I'll soon show you the videotapes. If this ever goes out to the rest of Pandora's Acolytes, I'll be damned because the Director doesn't like it when people go around his stuff. He usually keeps it neatly organized on his desk and its drawers. God knows what he does in the office while he's not out. Has he met God? I mean, if he can come back to life, then it must mean he has, right?

If someone ever really does release this, please make it Level-PSI, because I really don't want to be dealing with repercussions from the Director. He might even cook me alive if it happens. But knowing only he will have access to this already eases me. Even then the gavel of time might hit Nex someday, although I don't want him to stay dead. These past years have been really weird ever since Gamma mysteriously died. She was really important to Pandora's Acolytes history.

Nex's beloved wife "Katherine Woods," from what I've eavesdropped, seems like she's doing some charity campaigns and donations, and it doesn't seem to me that they have any children yet. But Katherine has also been doing some shady deals, as in dealing anomalous weapons, but she's the one making the weapons. It's weird. But from what I've seen, she does a work of art in making those weapons. She may not seem anomalous or abnormal, but she does have some expertise crafting weapons that are going for sale. She and Nex usually dress like rich people in public. Although I've never seen Katherine around the main HQ, she might be on the other camps also making special weapons for Pandora's Acolytes personnel.

From the last raid we've done on Site-98, I was attacked by Foundation personnel, they were wearing green from what I remember, eventually being captured by them. They asked me some questions, about who I was, who led Pandora's Acolytes, about the armbands we wear, and stuff like that, before I somehow started hearing things. While I was being interrogated, I heard something in my head say "The bird is watching." I thought it was some random thing in my head, but then I heard it mention the Director. It started telling me to not give any info and such. But after some time, someone decided to give me a chance, a second chance. They didn't use amnestics on me, luckily, and I was able to act as a double agent for Pandora's Acolytes. After that "bird is watching" thing, I assume it was one of Nex's superpowers.

I sometimes think that Nex isn't the real owner of the company, and that there's something bigger behind it, something left in time long ago. But if that's the case, then who is the real owner of the company? I remember surfing on the internet, reading news articles, and I saw something about a family that had mysteriously died, probably the same way as Gamma did, but their names were Bevere. Something. They had this small family-owned company based in Russia, Siberia. People thought it was some random farm, but how could it be a farm if the weather conditions for Siberia were horrible for the part they were in? And why would it be a farm if Pandora's Acolytes headquarters is located close to that one single farm place? I think I got it. The Bevere family are the original owners of Pandora's Acolytes. But I'm pretty sure they had a similar name to this one, something like Pandora's Protocol. But it doesn't even matter because the names are pretty close.

I have also heard of this one KillSquad division within Pandora's Acolytes. I forgot what their name was. But it does seem like it also had our main name on it, it was like Pando... Pandorae? With another word before Pandorae. I heard they're professional as hell when it comes to dealing with death, if you know what I mean. They closely protect the Directors and their secretaries, but they're very limited too. I haven't really seen them aside from our special operators.

[ADDENDUM #1]

[???]: Ugh...
[The Bird]: Do you know where you are?
[???]: No...
[The person would look around]

INTERNAL NOTE: If this document is ever accessed without Level-PSI clearance, the person responsible will be dealing with the Director personally. This warning was placed here at his request.
